meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity Project Template for SaaS MVPs
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS. Use our Antigravity project template and Claude Code to launch faster, validate revenue, and finish your MVP with expert precision.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' syndrome is a failure of architecture, not willpower. By using the Antigravity project template, you enforce a modular, revenue-first structure that forces progress. Combined with Claude Code, you automate boilerplate and focus purely on business logic, allowing you to ship your MVP in days, not months.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: a graveyard of half-baked repositories, abandoned side projects, and 'perfect' codebases that never saw the light of a production environment. The friction isn't your coding ability; it’s the lack of a systemic framework that protects you from scope creep and architectural paralysis. You aren't just building an app; you are building a business, and every day spent in development limbo is a day you aren't collecting revenue.

It is time to stop tinkering and start shipping. The Antigravity project template acts as your technical scaffolding, providing the hardened patterns necessary to move from prototype to payment-ready SaaS. When you integrate this with Claude Code, you effectively hire an autonomous engineering lead that keeps your velocity high and your focus on the features that actually convert users into customers.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffolding
steps.description: Establish a production-grade foundation that includes authentication, database schema, and payment integration from minute one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new SaaS project using the Antigravity core template with Stripe checkout and Supabase Auth enabled.

steps.title: Define the Revenue-Driving Feature
steps.description: Isolate the 'Core Value Proposition' to prevent scope creep. Only build what the user will pay for immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the business goal: [INSERT GOAL]. Generate the minimal viable feature set required for a paid beta launch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify the MVP feature set for this project'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate with Claude Code
steps.description: Offload the repetitive task of writing CRUD operations and API wrappers to Claude Code to save cognitive load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the API endpoints and database models based on the core feature set defined in step 2.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path src/api/features --intent 'generate CRUD for user-facing features'

steps.title: Implement Rapid Feedback Loops
steps.description: Connect your frontend to the backend immediately to validate the user flow and identify friction points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create the UI components for the core feature, ensuring they are bound to the Antigravity state management system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ui --generate 'create responsive dashboard for feature X'

steps.title: Deployment and Revenue Validation
steps.description: Deploy to a staging environment to test real-world interactions and ensure the payment gateway is live.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for production-ready hosting and verify Stripe test mode keys.

pro_tips: Treat your MVP like a perishable good; if it isn't deployed in 14 days, kill the feature, not the project.
Use Claude Code to audit your own code for 'over-engineering'—if a function is more than 30 lines, refactor it immediately.
Hard-code your monetization points early; if you can't imagine a user clicking 'Buy' on a specific screen, that screen shouldn't exist yet.
Always use the Antigravity middleware for logging; knowing how users interact with your MVP is more valuable than the code itself.
Prioritize 'fast-fail' UI: use Claude Code to generate standard components rather than building custom CSS from scratch.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, run a 48-hour feedback loop with 5 potential users. Use the data collected to iterate on the core feature, then use Claude Code to implement the requested changes within a single sprint.
content.conversion_block: You are sitting on a goldmine of potential, but execution is the only currency that matters in the AI era. Stop dreaming about the perfect app and start building the one that pays your bills. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
